data.title: 'Time to Step Back,' Says Top Analyst About BigBear.ai Stock (BBAI)
data.description:
BigBear.ai Holdings (BBAI) stock has drawn caution from Wall Street as competition in the AI software space heats up. In a new research note, top Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Jonathan Ruykhaver downgraded BigBear.ai from Overweight to Neutral and lowered his price target to $6 from $7, implying about 3% downside from current levels.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why Cantor Turned Cautious on BBAI Stock

Cantor's downgrade came as the firm took a closer look at several software and AI stocks facing a tougher market. In BigBear.ai's case, the pressure is building from more than one direction.

The 5-star analyst said competition is picking up, with larger and better-known rivals pushing harder for the same customers. As that pressure grows, it becomes harder for BigBear.ai to hold pricing and protect margins.

While the analyst acknowledged BigBear.ai's role in AI-driven analytics, he prefers to stay on the sidelines for now. Until competition eases or execution improves, Cantor believes a Neutral rating better reflects the balance between the company's longer-term opportunity and the near-term headwinds it faces.

It is worth noting that Ruykhaver ranks 179 out of more than 10,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has a success rate of 61%, with an average return per rating of 22.3% over a one-year timeframe.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/9/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Slip ahead of Tariff Ruling and Jobs Report
data.description:
U.S. stock futures hovered near the flatline early Friday as markets awaited a possible U.S. Supreme Court decision on President Donald Trump's tariffs and Friday's jobs report. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.01%, 0.00%, and 0.03%, respectively, at 3:47 a.m. EST on January 9.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major averages closed mixed, as investors pulled back from high-flying tech stocks amid profit-taking and rising concerns over elevated valuations in the AI sector. The Nasdaq Composite dipped 0.44%, primarily weighed down by sharp losses in key chip and software names like Nvidia (NVDA), Palantir (PLTR), and Broadcom (AVGO). Meanwhile, the DJIA climbed approximately 270 points, or 0.55%, buoyed by gains in industrials and financials, and the S&P 500 eked out a negligible gain of less than 0.01%.

Despite these mixed sessions, U.S. stocks remain poised for weekly gains amid broader optimism from solid economic data and expectations for steady Federal Reserve policy. The S&P 500 is up about 0.9% for the week so far, while the Dow and Nasdaq have gained roughly 1.8% and 1.1%, respectively.

Looking ahead, investors will closely watch key economic reports on inflation expectations and the December jobs report that is expected to show slight gains. Nonfarm payrolls likely rose by 73,000 jobs last month, with unemployment ticking down to 4.5%, according to Dow Jones economist surveys.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.18%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $58.23 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,473 per ounce on Friday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mostly higher on Friday, buoyed by strong gains in defense stocks amid heightened geopolitical tensions and signals of elevated U.S. defense spending under President Trump's administration.

 Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly higher on January 9, propelled by U.S. signals for a substantially elevated defense budget and China's inflation hitting a three-year high, signaling economic resilience.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 0.32%. In China, the Shanghai Composite gained 0.92%, while the Shenzhen Component added 1.64%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei grew 1.61%, and the Topix gained 0.85%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Musk's Behavior Remains "Unacceptable" Despite Limiting Grok Image Editing Rights to Subscribers
data.description:
Elon Musk's behavior was slammed as "unacceptable" today as his social media platform X limited the ability of its users to create images in the wake of criticism over sexually explicit and violent pictures on the site.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Paying Subscribers Only

Posting on X, Grok, Musk's xAI tool, said that image generation and editing was now "currently limited to paying subscribers."

It comes in the wake of threats of fines, regulatory moves and a potential ban on the site because of "appalling images" being created by users on X.

Hannah Swirsky, head of policy at the Internet Watch Foundation, told the BBC that Musk's move "does not undo the harm which has been done." She added: "Sitting and waiting for unsafe products to be abused before taking action is unacceptable."

There are also concerns that there is a separate Grok app still available where non-paying users have reported being able to generate sexualized imagery of women and children.

Pressure Builds on Musk

According to a recent 24-hour review from January 5 to January 6, outside research found Grok produced about 6,700 altered images per hour. By comparison, the largest standalone sites focused on similar content produced about 79 images per hour in the same timeframe. Most images show women edited into bikinis or sheer outfits rather than full nudity, and in many cases, users triggered the images by tagging Grok under photos with short prompts.

Following these reports, regulators moved quickly across several regions. The UK regulator Ofcom said it contacted X and xAI to review legal duties under the Online Safety Act. France referred the issue to prosecutors, while India issued a 72-hour order to remove content and submit a report.

Yesterday, the European Commission added to the pressure on Musk, ordering X to keep all of its internal documents and data relating to the xAI Grok chatbot until the end of this year given fears over images on the platform.

Musk, arguably best known for his role as head of EV maker Tesla (TSLA), has defended Grok and said illegal use would face penalties. Limiting the function just to subscribers should make it easier for X to punish users given that they will know their personal and contact details.

data.author: David Craik

data.title: Bitcoin and Ether ETFs Suffer a Billion Dollar Heart Attack as Early Inflows Vanish
data.description:
The celebration for the new year was short-lived for the biggest names in crypto. After a promising start to 2026, U.S. spot Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and Ether (ETH-USD) ETFs have seen a massive reversal, with more than $1 billion exiting the funds since Tuesday. This sudden exit has wiped out almost all the gains made in the first few days of January. According to data from SoSoValue, Bitcoin ETFs alone saw about $1.13 billion in redemptions, proving that the early-year confidence was much more fragile than many had hoped.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Institutional Giants Retreat from the Market Fog

This billion-dollar drain is part of a larger trend of caution that started after the market reset in late 2025. Investors seem to be reassessing their risks after a volatile period where billions in leverage were wiped out. While July 2025 was a peak for buying, with Bitcoin funds seeing over $6 billion in a single month, the momentum has clearly cooled. Analysts believe this "controlled deleveraging" is a sign that the era of easy money is over, and institutional players are now much more careful about where they park their cash.

Altcoins Anchor the Market amid the Storm

In a surprising twist, not every part of the crypto ETF world is seeing red. While the giants are bleeding cash, funds tracking altcoins like XRP and Solana have managed to stay in the green. These smaller ETFs have seen steady, though smaller, inflows since they launched in late 2025. This suggests that some investors aren't leaving the crypto space entirely; instead, they are rotating their money into more specific bets rather than sticking with the broader Bitcoin and Ether markets.

Interestingly, the big move out of these funds is happening just as banks and government officials are watching crypto more carefully. The excitement of starting a new year has already faded, and now everyone is focused on whether these ETFs can survive the constant price swings. To stay strong, the people running these funds need to prove that the market can remain steady over the next few months.

Investors can track the prices of their favorite cryptos on the TipRanks Cryptocurrency Center. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: AI Adoption Could Lift These 2 Stocks Higher, Says Truist
data.description:
It has been just over three years since OpenAI launched ChatGPT, setting off a rapid acceleration in generative AI development. The technology is often compared to past inflection points such as the Industrial Revolution or the rise of cloud computing – not because the parallels are perfect, but because each reshaped how work is done and where economic value is created.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Generative AI is now pushing directly into the information economy. It is automating many of the behind-the-scenes tasks that underpin digital work, spanning software development, finance, design, and customer service, and in the process reducing the amount of human labor required in these areas.

While disruptions among high-skill digital workers are already emerging and may intensify, the productivity gains are equally hard to ignore, as companies find they can operate faster and at greater scale with leaner teams. Regardless of one's view on these shifts, their economic and social consequences are no longer hypothetical.

Analyst Arvind Ramnani, from Truist, has been watching this situation develop, and ably sums up where we're going: "With GenAI becoming the new general purpose tech layer of the economy, we expect its adoption to compress the market impact of the last 40-years of enterprise tech into the next 5-10 years… As frontier-model platforms scale and enterprises incorporate copilots and agents into their workflows, it will likely result in lower payroll intensity per unit of output, higher productivity of human capital, and new software-like business models built on usage-based AI services rather than traditional licenses."

"We believe that the economic rent pool will shift toward model providers, infra vendors, and application companies that can convert raw capabilities into domain specific systems, giving the GenAI ecosystem an outsized claim on future profit growth," the analyst added.

Ramnani goes on to pick two AI stocks – each an AI-powered digital platform – that he believes will rise higher as AI adoption expands. We've pulled the latest data from TipRanks to get a feel for where these stocks stand on Wall Street; here's a closer look at them.

Duolingo (DUOL)

The first Truist pick on our radar is Duolingo, the popular language-learning app. Most of us are familiar with Duolingo – the app has found a wide audience for its language education products, and for good reason. The digital world is growing more and more interconnected, and having a basic knowledge of languages beyond one's own mother tongue provides an increasingly important advantage. Duolingo has tapped into that, and offers courses designed for an audience that wants to learn the basics of another language but does not necessarily have the time or resources for a formal language course.

Duolingo's courses are offered online and lessons can be found in dozens of languages – more than 40, at the last count – through some 280 courses. And in addition, the company also offers courses in music, math, and chess – in fact, chess has become the fastest growing course on the site.

The core product, however, remains the language courses. This is where Duolingo excels. Users can find such major tongues as English, Chinese, French, Spanish, and Arabic, as well as courses in Japanese, German, Dutch – it's a long list. Smaller languages, such as Zulu, Navajo, or Yiddish, are also included, as well as 'specialty' or fantasy languages like Klingon.

All of this has powered Duolingo's growth, which has been substantial. Duolingo launched in 2012, and in the years since it has become the world's leading mobile language-learning app. As of the end of 3Q25, the company boasted over 50 million daily active users, and more than 135 million monthly active users. These figures were up 36% and 20% year-over-year, respectively. Paid subscribers, at 11.5 million, were up 34% year-over-year.

Duolingo generated $271.7 million in revenue during 3Q25, a total that was up an impressive 41% year-over-year – and that beat the forecast by $11.36 million. At the bottom line, the company realized $5.95 in EPS; we should note that this figure, which exceeded expectations by $5.19 per share, was heavily skewed by a one-time income tax benefit recorded in the quarter.

We should also note that Duolingo's stock has been falling in recent months. Recent factors affecting the drop include weaker than expected Q4 guidance on bookings, and a strategic shift by the company to favor long-term growth over short-term gains – both factors indicate a potential slow-down in the coming months.

In his write-up on Duolingo, analyst Ramnani focuses on the company's extensive use of AI technology as a firm support for a quality app, stating, "The company is using GenAI for content generation and has notably launched 140+ new language courses over the past year vs. 100+ over 12 years prior. The company is also already utilizing GenAI coding tools to develop new products, and we believe its deep user data and expertise in gamifying learning/engaging users will continue to provide defensible advantages even as AI natives enter the market… We see continued opportunity for DUOL to grow through its language courses as 2B+ people are looking to learn a 2nd language. We believe DUOL's expansion into new subjects is a significant growth lever, with potential for new monetization streams over time."

Following from this, Ramnani puts a Buy rating on DUOL shares, along with a $245 price target that points to a one-year upside potential of 41%. (To watch Ramnani's track record, click here)

Duolingo stock holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Wall Street, based on 17 reviews that include 10 to Buy, 6 to Hold, and 1 to Sell. The stock is priced at $173.87 and its $260.36 average price target implies a gain of 50% by the end of this year. (See DUOL stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: M&A News: Revolution Medicines (RVMD) Stock Rallies on News of Potential Acquisition by Merck
data.description:
Shares of biotech Revolution Medicines (RVMD) rallied more than 13% in Friday's pre-market trading, after rising 4.6% yesterday in reaction to news of a potential acquisition by pharma giant Merck (MRK). Citing sources, the Financial Times reported that Merck has not finalized a deal yet and that a potential transaction could take at least several weeks.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, earlier this week, AbbVie (ABBV) denied that it is in talks to acquire Revolution Medicines, following a report by the Wall Street Journal that the drugmaker was in advanced discussions to acquire the cancer-focused biotech.

Revolution Medicines Sees Takeover Buzz

According to the Financial Times, the potential acquisition of Revolution Medicines by Merck is valued in the range of $28 billion to $32 billion. If the deal goes through, it would be the largest healthcare acquisition since Pfizer (PFE) took over cancer biotech Seagen for $43 billion.

Reportedly, other large pharma companies are also interested in acquiring Revolution Medicines. RVMD's pipeline is focused on innovative treatments targeting RAS-addicted cancers.

Several pharma giants are seeking attractive targets, as they face the risk of patent expirations and competition from generics. In particular, Merck has been making strategic acquisitions ahead of the loss of patent for its blockbuster cancer treatment Keytruda. Last year, the company bought flu prevention company Cidara Therapeutics for $9.2 billion and respiratory drug company Verona Pharma.

Is RVMD a Good Stock to Buy?

Remarkably, Revolution Medicines scores Wall Street's Strong Buy consensus rating based on 20 unanimous Buys. The average RVMD stock price target of $81.88 indicates 24% downside risk from current levels. As of Thursday's closing, RVMD stock has risen 142% over the past year.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Anthropic Expands Enterprise Reach with German Insurer Allianz
data.description:
Anthropic, the company behind the Claude chatbot and a key rival to ChatGPT maker OpenAI, has further broadened its enterprise footprint with a new partnership with German insurance giant Allianz (ALIZY).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On Friday, the company, which is backed by tech giants such as Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet (GOOGL), disclosed that it has teamed up with Allianz "to accelerate the adoption of responsible artificial intelligence" at the insurance company.

The deal adds Allianz to Anthropic's prior partnerships with Snowflake (SNOW), Accenture (ACN), Palantir (PLTR), and Amazon Web Services. The startup also recently secured a combined investment commitment of up to $15 billion from Microsoft (MSFT) and Nvidia ($NVDA) that involves Microsoft's Azure cloud services.

Anthropic-Allianz Alliance to Improve Insurance AI

As part of Anthropic's new partnership with Allianz, employees at the insurance company will adopt Anthropic's Claude AI models even as both firms work together to build custom AI agents. These agents will automate Allianz's labor-intensive activities at scale, from document intake to insurance claims processing.

In addition, the two firms are working together to develop AI systems tailored for compliance with the insurance industry's regulatory requirements. "With this partnership, Allianz is taking a decisive step to address critical AI challenges in insurance," noted Oliver Bäte, Allianz's chief executive.

Anthropic Eyes the Health Industry

Meanwhile, the new collaboration also comes as Anthropic plans to showcase the capacity of its Claude model for the healthcare and life sciences industries. This is even as rival OpenAI has rolled out a new feature called ChatGPT Health. Microsoft has also made recent moves in this space.

Anthropic remains focused on expanding its financial base and is reportedly planning to raise about $10 billion in new funding at a valuation of about $350 billion. The startup is expected to go public this year.

data.author: Solomon Oladipupo
